response.overview: The film begins at Arnarhóll, where children are sledding. The rural romance floats over the lakes, and the lyrics suggest that children, some of whom get to enjoy a stay in the countryside in the summer, recall their connection with the animals and wander down to the southern end of the pond, Þorfinnstjörn, in search of peace. Hundreds of ducks of ten different species have nested there. It is traced how they raise their young in the nearby puddles and marshes and then bring them to the pond, where the children give them bits of their nest on their way to and from school.
response.title: Fuglarnir okkar
